##NAMES=Cuyaite
##RRUFFID=R250043
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Mn^3+^As^3+^_14_O_24_Cl
##LOCALITY=Cuya, Camarones Valley, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Divergent sprays and subparallel intergrowths of thin pale brown needles associated with anhydrite, native arsenic, arsenolite, calcite, claudetite, ferrinatrite, gajardoite-3R, leiteite, magnesiocopiapite, phosphosiderite, pyrite, realgar and talmessite. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2020)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250043
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Ca_2.03_Mn^3+^_0.95_(As^3+^_13.66_Sb^3+^_0.65_)O_24_Cl_0.88_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 14.7231 b: 5.58709 c: 17.4185 alpha: 90 beta: 112.451 gamma: 90 volume: 1324.23 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
